enonce.texte:
Complete each sentence with the correct preposition from the box. This exercise tests your understanding of common preposition usage in English.

Prepositions: at, in, on, by, for

questions.texte: I will meet you ______ the bus stop.
She was born ______ October 15, 2005.
They live ______ a small village near Douala.
I sent the letter ______ my sister.
She learned English ______ watching movies.
The meeting is scheduled ______ 9:00 AM.
He has been waiting ______ an hour.
The book is ______ the table.

intitule: Identifying Prepositions of Time and Place

enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully. Identify whether the underlined preposition indicates TIME or PLACE. Write your answer clearly.

questions.texte: We arrived <u>at</u> the airport at 7 PM.
She will start her new job <u>in</u> January.
The students are sitting <u>on</u> the grass.
They met <u>at</u> 5:30 PM.
The children are playing <u>in</u> the garden.
We go to church <u>on</u> Sundays.

enonce.texte: Choose the correct conjunction to complete each sentence. This exercise will help you master the use of conjunctions in English.

questions.texte: She wanted to go to the party, ______ she was too tired.
______ it was raining, they decided to go for a walk.
He studied hard, ______ he passed the exam.
She missed the bus; ______, she was late for work.
I like both dogs ______ cats.
______ she was tired, she continued working.
She wanted to buy the dress, ______ it was too expensive.

enonce.texte:
Read the following paragraph about daily life in Cameroon. Fill in the blanks with the correct prepositions: at, in, on, by, to, for, with, from

Life in a Cameroonian village is often peaceful and community-oriented. People usually wake up (1) ______ dawn and start their daily activities. The women go (2) ______ the market (3) ______ the morning to sell fresh vegetables and fruits. Children walk (4) ______ school (5) ______ foot, carrying their books and notebooks. (6) ______ the afternoon, many people rest (7) ______ the shade of large trees. In the evening, families gather together (8) ______ dinner and share stories about their day.

questions.texte: Fill in blank (1): People usually wake up ______ dawn.
Fill in blank (2): The women go ______ the market.
Fill in blank (3): The women go to the market ______ the morning.
Fill in blank (4): Children walk ______ school.
Fill in blank (5): Children walk to school ______ foot.
Fill in blank (6): ______ the afternoon, many people rest.
Fill in blank (7): Many people rest ______ the shade of large trees.
Fill in blank (8): Families gather together ______ dinner.

enonce.texte: Combine each pair of sentences using the conjunction provided in brackets. Write the complete sentence.

questions.texte: He was tired. He continued working. (although)
questions.reponse: Although he was tired, he continued working.

questions.texte: She did not study. She passed the exam. (however)
questions.reponse: She did not study; however, she passed the exam.

questions.texte: It was raining. We went to the market. (although)
questions.reponse: Although it was raining, we went to the market.

questions.texte: He is poor. He is very generous. (but)
questions.reponse: He is poor, but he is very generous.

questions.texte: The students worked hard. They passed their exams. (so)
questions.reponse: The students worked hard, so they passed their exams.

questions.texte: She had no money. She couldn't buy the book. (because)
questions.reponse: She couldn't buy the book because she had no money.

enonce.texte: Each sentence contains an incorrect preposition. Identify the error and correct it.

questions.texte: She is interested <u>on</u> learning English.
He is married <u>with</u> a beautiful woman.
I am tired <u>from</u> this long journey.
She depends <u>from</u> her parents for support.
He is good <u>at</u> mathematics.
I will meet you <u>in</u> the corner of the street.
questions.reponse: meet you at the corner of the street.

enonce.texte:
Read the following passage about school life in Cameroon. Choose the correct preposition or conjunction to complete the text.

School life in Cameroon is both challenging and rewarding. Students wake up early (1) ______ the morning and go to school (2) ______ bus or on foot. (3) ______ they arrive, they attend classes (4) ______ different subjects. The school day usually ends (5) ______ 3:30 PM. (6) ______ students face difficulties, (7) ______ they remain determined to succeed. Many students study hard, (8) ______ they want to have a better future.

questions.texte: Choose the correct option for blank (1): Students wake up early ______ the morning.
Choose the correct option for blank (2): Students go to school ______ bus or on foot.
Choose the correct option for blank (3): ______ they arrive, they attend classes.
Choose the correct option for blank (4): They attend classes ______ different subjects.
Choose the correct option for blank (5): The school day usually ends ______ 3:30 PM.
Choose the correct option for blank (6): ______ students face difficulties, they remain determined.
Choose the correct option for blank (7): Students face difficulties, ______ they remain determined.
Choose the correct option for blank (8): Many students study hard, ______ they want to have a better future.

enonce.texte: Write a paragraph of about 100-120 words describing your daily routine or a memorable experience. Use at least five different conjunctions and five different prepositions correctly.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here:
questions.reponse:
Model Answer:

My typical day starts early in the morning at 5:30 AM. I wake up by the sound of my alarm clock, but sometimes I wake up before it rings because I am used to waking up early. After taking a shower, I have breakfast with my family around 6:00 AM. Although I don't like eating too early, I know it is important for my health. I go to school by bus, and I usually arrive at school around 7:30 AM. During the day, I attend classes in different subjects, including English, Mathematics, and History. In the afternoon, I often stay at the library to study because I want to pass my exams. When I return home, I help my parents with household chores, and then I do my homework until dinner time. On weekends, I enjoy playing football with my friends, for it is my favorite sport.
